title: Heatwave conditions intensify across multiple districts - Bangalore Mirror
full_text:
Heatwave conditions intensify across multiple districts
.
The summer heat has intensified sharply across Karnataka, with temperatures crossing 40°C in several districts, leaving people distressed. Kalaburagi recorded 41.6°C, while Gadag, Raichur, Vijayapura and Yadgir hovered around 40°C, signalling heatwave-like conditions across the Kalyana Karnataka region. Residents report that intense heat begins as early as 8:30 am and continues well into the evening, disrupting daily routines. Street vendors and daily wage workers remain among the worst affected, as high humidity and relentless sunlight make outdoor work increasingly difficult.
Experts warn that temperatures above 40°C pose serious health risks and advise people, especially the elderly, to avoid unnecessary exposure. Coastal districts have also seen unusual heat, with temperatures touching 34.7°C and likely to rise further. While no heavy rainfall is expected in Dakshina Kannada, Udupi and Kasaragod, thunderstorms may develop after April 15. Meanwhile, parts of Hassan, Kodagu and Mysuru may receive light to moderate rain, and Bengaluru is likely to remain partly cloudy at 36°C.
